formations_blocks: Bosnia in a defensive low block / deep shape
Switzerland in sustained possession, territorial dominance, and early corner pressure
adjustments: Embolo dropping deep to receive in midfield
Bosnia's back line stepping up to catch Embolo offside
Switzerland using short-corner routines and left-sided build-up rather than direct crosses
key_matchups: Widmer defending Alajbegovic's early left-sided delivery
Muharemovic versus Embolo in the box
Dedic under pressure and caught in possession by Switzerland's press/build-up
Akanji's forward passing against Bosnia's defensive line
phases.summary: Switzerland controlled the ball and the territory from the outset, pinning Bosnia back with repeated attacks down the left and a series of corners. Bosnia’s early threat came mainly from set pieces and a left-sided delivery toward Dzeko, but they offered little sustained possession and increasingly struggled to clear their lines. Switzerland looked most dangerous when Embolo dropped into pockets or made inside-channel runs, while Bosnia’s attempt to step up led to at least one offside trap and some early defensive disorder.

formations_blocks: Bosnia defending in a committed low block / under sustained pressure
adjustments: Ndoye repeatedly shifting from the left touchline into central areas and the box
Widmer advancing into free space on the right side and inside the box
Switzerland recycling possession back to the centre-backs when direct routes are blocked
key_matchups: Dedic versus Ndoye down the Swiss left
Memic versus Rodriguez on the halfway line
phases.summary: Switzerland controlled the ball for long spells and moved it quickly, but Bosnia stayed organized and committed defensively, limiting clear shooting opportunities despite heavy pressure. The most notable duel was Dedic against Ndoye on Switzerland’s left, while Widmer and Xhaka helped sustain the attacking pattern as Switzerland recycled possession to keep Bosnia penned in.

Switzerland had the majority of possession, but it was largely sterile: 73% of the ball produced only four shots to Bosnia and Herzegovina’s three, with most efforts coming from distance. Xhaka was singled out as the standout performer, but the overall story was one of dominance without penetration, leaving the game level at 0-0 at half-time.

formations_blocks: Bosnia in a disciplined defensive shape, effectively sitting in a low/mid block
Switzerland pushing higher with more aggressive attacking occupation of the final third
adjustments: Switzerland increased their tempo after a flat restart
Ndoye was used more directly on the left wing, cutting inside to shoot
Ekanji stepped high up the pitch to initiate attacks from deep
key_matchups: Embolo versus Katic and Bosnia's double marking
Ekanji versus Dzeko in the build-up to the corner
phases.summary: The first few minutes of the half saw Bosnia maintain a compact, disciplined defensive block while Switzerland initially lacked sharpness and were easily contained. Switzerland then raised the intensity, with Ekanji advancing from deep, Ndoye repeatedly threatening from the left and through shots, and Embolo being closely marked to deny central access. The phase was defined by Swiss territorial pressure and a series of half-chances rather than a clear structural change, with Bosnia mainly trying to absorb pressure and keep the game closed.

formations_blocks: Bosnia & Herzegovina defending in a low block after the game opened up
Switzerland pressing forward in control after going a man up
adjustments: Switzerland's substitutes added impact, especially Vargas down the left and Manzambi up front/attacking the box
Bosnia & Herzegovina went down to 10 men after Muharemovic’s red-card foul on Manzambi
Switzerland increased their pressure for a killer goal following the sending-off
key_matchups: Vargas vs the Bosnian right side/down the left channel
Vasilj vs Vargas/Switzerland’s set-piece and follow-up pressure
phases.summary: This phase turned decisively once the game opened up and Switzerland’s substitutes exploited the space, especially down the left. Manzambi’s spectacular volley gave Switzerland the lead, and Muharemovic’s red-card foul then left Bosnia & Herzegovina exposed; from there Switzerland controlled the match and added two more goals before a late Bosnian consolation and a final Swiss strike.
